channel.title: RSS Aggregator Feed
channel.description: An aggregated feed of Technology-related news

item.title: Nintendo cuts Switch 2 production amid weakening console sales
item.description: Nintendo will produce fewer Switch 2 units than it previously projected in response to weakening demand for the console in the US, Bloomberg reports. Four million units of Nintendo's flagship device are expected to be made this quarter instead of the six million it had originally planned, according to unnamed sources cited by the publication, […]

item.title: ‘AI is not here to replace expertise, it's here to elevate’: Oracle CEO says the best still to come when it comes to agentic innovation
item.description: Oracle CEO outlines his company’s ongoing plan to build “the world's largest AI cloud”.

item.title: When AI drives the M&A deal, reality arrives fast
item.description: Why AI-led acquisitions expose hidden costs, complexity, and integration risk faster than expected.

item.title: PS5 Pro architect Mark Cerny says Sony's updated PSSR tech is 'something like 100 microseconds faster than the original'
item.description: PS5 and PS5 Pro console architect Mark Cerny has revealed that Sony’s updated PSSR (PlayStation Spectral Super Resolution) AI upscaling technology is faster than the original.

item.title: Clear Drop Soft Plastic Compactor Review: Eco Experiment
item.description: Clear Drop’s Soft Plastic Compactor is a unique household recycling solution that’s fun to use, but who is it for?

item.title: LG Display porte sur PC portables l’une des plus grandes avancées énergétique de nos smartphones
item.description: LG Display annonce cette semaine être le premier fabricant de dalles LCD à produire en masse des panneaux capables de passer dynamiquement de 1 à 120 Hz sur PC portables. Une petite révolution pour nos laptops, dans le sillage de ce que l'on connaît déjà sur smartphones.

item.title: Google Messages signe la fin du QR code pour la version web, voici comment vous connecter
item.description: Google s'apprête à supprimer la connexion par QR code pour la version web de Messages, imposant désormais l'utilisation de votre compte Google pour synchroniser vos SMS.

item.title: C’est la fête : l’enceinte JBL d’une puissance de 240 W RMS capable d’ambiancer vos soirées est en promo à -109 €
item.description: JBL est la marque référente pour les enceintes Bluetooth, surtout pour faire la fête. C'est pour ça que la JBL PartyBox Stage 320 en promo à 490 euros au lieu de 599,99 euros sur Amazon est très intéressante.

item.title: ‘Warcraft… with pure thought control’ — 100 days with Neuralink ‘feels like science fiction’ to early brain chip pioneer
item.description: After 100 days with a Neuralink implant, a patient describes playing Warcraft with pure thought

item.title: The US just banned new routers that aren't made in America — here's what it means for your Wi-Fi network
item.description: US authorities have turned their attention to routers made outside the US, suggesting they're a national security risk.

item.title: What is the macOS Dock? Here’s how to master Apple’s taskbar rival on your brand-new MacBook Neo
item.description: Learn how the macOS Dock works with our complete guide for new Mac users.

item.title: The ‘Mandalorian and Grogu’ Hot Toys Figures Want You to Know Pedro Pascal Is Under That Helmet
item.description: Who needs Mandalorian cultural discourse when you could have a very pretty 12-inch replica of Pedro Pascal?

item.title: How to Use Apple’s Live Translation on Your AirPods
item.description: If you have a recent Apple device, you can translate live conversations. It’s impressive, and the new AirPods Pro Max just joined the party.

item.title: The most innovative retail companies of 2026
item.description: Why Shopify, Walmart, Fanatics, and J.Crew are among Fast Company’s Most Innovative Companies in retail for 2026. The most innovative retailers in 2025 used technology not to chase trends, but to solve real problems.

item.title: The most innovative companies in public relations and brand strategy for 2026
item.description: Fast Company’s 2025 list of the 10 most innovative companies in public relations and brand strategy includes Giant Spoon, Weber Shandwick, Jellyfish, Cole McVoy and more. The world of public relations has always been about making a splash. And in an age of more and more media clutter, breakthrough ideas have never been more important. To create that can’t-miss-it buzz, this year’s most innovative PR firms paired an A-list Hollywood actor with an A-list Hollywood director, staged a surprise pop-event in a major urban transportation hub, enlisted some of the biggest stars from the booming world ...

item.title: The most innovative companies in economic development for 2026
item.description: Fast Company’s 2026 list of the 10 most innovative organizations in economic development includes the state of New Mexico, the Next California/WWF, the San Diego Unified School District, Greater Hartford Gives Foundation, and more. Traditional economic development tends to focus on investments made and jobs created. For this year’s group of Most Innovative Companies—or in many cases, foundations or governments, in this case—the key performance indicator isn’t dollars spent, but connections made. Growth comes not from directing resources, but finding a better way to nurture what you already hav...

item.title: The most innovative companies in media and news for 2026
item.description: Fast Company’s 2026 list of the 10 most innovative companies in media and news includes Cloudflare, TBPN, the New York Times, The Onion, and more. The way we consume culture has fractured into millions of pieces and the far corners of the internet. But media companies are finding creative ways to keep capturing market share.For publishing imprint Bloom Books, that means capitalizing on TikTok’s rise by turning #BookTok’s viral hits into paperback bestsellers. For Webtoon, it’s doubling down on a dynamic fast-metabolism format with five-minute-long “episodes” that bring comic books to life. The...

item.title: The most innovative advertising and marketing companies of 2026
item.description: Why Adidas, Billie, FCB, and State Farm are among the most innovative ad and marketing companies of 2026. In some ways, the attention game for brands is only getting tougher. The increased pace of the cultural cycle and the tidal wave of slop hitting our feeds have added a layer of suspicion to any brand work. Is it real? How do you know?

item.title: The most innovative companies in agriculture for 2026
item.description: Why Dyson, Cargill, Holganix, and Fed by Blue are among Fast Company’s Most Innovative Companies in agriculture for 2026. Taking stock of the once red-hot agtech sector, analysts have called 2025 a “transition year,” a polite way of saying crop prices slid, Bayer traded near a 20-year low, John Deere reported less than half of its 2023 income, and almost two dozen startups in once-frothy areas like indoor farming, drones, and insect-based ingredients collapsed. It was enough for a managing director of ag giant Syngenta’s VC arm to jokingly “thank God” it had avoided investing in alt-protein, c...

item.title: The most innovative companies in applied AI for 2026
item.description: Why Sierra, Cursor, Coactive, Lovable, and ServiceNow are among Fast Company’s Most Innovative Companies of 2026. Three years (plus) after the arrival of ChatGPT, chatbots are morphing into AI agents. As generative AI models have improved and become able to reason in real time, the major AI labs, starting with Anthropic, have begun to shift their research focus from models that compose and comprehend text to ones that reason, use tools, and work autonomously. ...

item.title: The most innovative architecture companies of 2026
item.description: Why HKS, NBBJ, and Autodesk are among Fast Company’s Most Innovative Companies in architecture for 2026. Beyond the not insignificant work of designing buildings, it can often seem that architects are also tasked with solving some of the biggest problems in the world. From reducing the environmental impact of buildings to increasing access to affordable spaces to fighting climate change to rebuilding what climate change has damaged, the architect’s work can verge on the infinite....

item.title: The most innovative artificial intelligence companies of 2026
item.description: From big names such as Google and Anthropic to specialists like Abridge, World Labs, and Mithril, these companies are proving that AI’s ability to solve new problems is hardly tapped out. Over the past year, tech companies invested hundreds of billions in the new data centers needed to power rapidly increasing demand for the technology. The investment is motivated in part by confidence that major AI labs such as those at OpenAI, Anthropic, and Google will continue to wring more intelligence out of their models. Indeed, fears have receded that the AI labs’ go-to strategy of supersizing models, ...

item.title: The most innovative Asia-Pacific companies of 2026
item.description: Critical chip players TSMC and Tokyo Electron are joined by Indian climate tech startup Varaha and the South Korean maker of viral Buldak ramen on Fast Company’s Most Innovative Companies list this year. It should come as no surprise that the global chip wars that grabbed headlines over the past year made an impact at the top of the Asia-Pacific list. Taiwanese semiconductor giant TSMC, in the No. 1 position, has reinforced its role as an industry lynchpin, becoming the first to put hotly anticipated 2-nanometer chips into production. Tokyo Electron, which provides the specialized equipment f...

item.title: The most innovative augmented and virtual reality companies of 2026
item.description: Why Cosm, Xreal, Immotion, and XRHealth are among Fast Company’s Most Innovative Companies in augmented and virtual reality for 2026. Augmented and virtual reality companies continue to harness the technology for everything from family entertainment to healthcare and workplace safety. Xreal’s wearable displays offer users new options for integrating AR with workflows and devices, and RayNeo’s ultralight AR glasses deliver AI features and a stunning 43-inch virtual display. AR and VR are even creating innovative forms of entertainment. Cosm has built “shared reality” domes that let spectators i...

item.title: The most innovative automotive companies of 2026
item.description: Why Waymo, Baidu, Hyundai, and Cadillac are among Fast Company’s Most Innovative Companies in automotive for 2026. Automotive companies paving the way for self-driving cars are changing the rules of the road.

item.title: The most innovative companies in beauty for 2026
item.description: Why Rhode, Hourglass, Sephora, and Dazzle Dry are among Fast Company’s Most Innovative Companies in beauty this year. As technology accelerates so many aspects of life, consumers are expecting the same from their beauty products. Speed, ease, and efficacy are paramount in their minds.

item.title: The most innovative biotech companies of 2026
item.description: Why Enveda, Faeth Therapeutics, Gilgamesh Pharma, and Argo Biopharma are among the most innovative companies in biotech right now. The Most Innovative Companies in biotech reflect a year in which companies took big swings—delivering breakthrough treatments to the clinic, bringing groundbreaking tests to the public, and building out the tools to support future breakthroughs....

item.title: The most innovative business services companies for 2026
item.description: Why Dell, QuickFi, HubSpot, and Schneider Electric are among Fast Company’s Most Innovative Companies in business services for 2026. Bet you’ll never guess the main theme running through all 10 of this year’s Most Innovative Companies honorees in the business services category. Yep: artificial intelligence.

item.title: The most innovative computing companies of 2026
item.description: Why Nvidia, Amazon Web Services, Google, and xLight are among Fast Company’s Most Innovative Companies in computing for 2026. The most innovative companies in computing this year reflect wide-ranging efforts to build the infrastructure that next-generation AI applications require, and to enable their deployment in virtually any imaginable location or scenario. Nvidia’s GB300 NVL72 platform, an “AI supercomputer”—incorporating GPUs, CPUs, power, cooling and networking components—delivers 50x the reasoning power of prior systems. It is already being deployed by hyperscalers and cloud platforms s...

item.title: The most innovative companies in consumer and household goods for 2026
item.description: Why Tide, L’Oréal, Unilever, and Hoto are among Fast Company’s Most Innovative Companies in consumer and household goods this year. All great household products make our lives at home easier. The truly next-level ones solve problems we didn’t know we had. This year’s most innovative companies in consumer goods did that by delivering a range of kitchen items, bathroom essentials, and everyday gadgets that make the products we swear by even better....

item.title: The most innovative consumer electronics companies of 2026
item.description: From AMD and Framework’s potent AI rigs to Eight Sleep’s aids for better slumber, these companies are applying fresh thinking to a bevy of consumer categories. This year’s most compelling consumer electronics innovations are all about making technology more empowering, with fewer annoying compromises.

item.title: The companies with the most innovative corporate social responsibility programs of 2026
item.description: The corporate social responsibility efforts of Microsoft, Lego, Babylist, and Samsung are among some of Fast Company’s most innovative in 2026. During a year when many companies retreated from DEI initiatives—or, at the very least, stopped touting them—others held firm. Proving that the “responsibility” aspect of corporate social responsibility is a strategic choice, the most innovative companies find ways to extend the impact of their core businesses—or tread into new waters....

item.title: The most innovative data science companies of 2026
item.description: Why Unstructured, Feedzai, Synchron, and Chalk are among Fast Company’s Most Innovative Companies in data science for 2026. AI innovations have long promised productivity at scale, powered by breakthroughs in underlying technologies such as large language models (LLMs), aiding state-of-the-art applications to reason with remarkable fluency. Yet as AI adoption deepens, the constraint is no longer what models can do, but the data science and analytics foundations they depend on. Across industries, the data infrastructure feeding modern AI still resembles digital filing cabinets. Critical informa...

item.title: La Switch 2 se vendrait un peu moins bien ces derniers temps, et c’est normal
item.description: Après un début triomphal, la Switch 2 connaît une petite baisse de régime en ce début d’année 2026. Pas de quoi inquiéter Nintendo outre mesure, cela dit. Les prévisions restent encourageantes.

item.title: 912 chevaux et 0 à 100 en 2,8 s : Lynk & Co dégaine une voiture électrique ultra-performante, la Z10+
item.description: Le constructeur chinois Lynk & Co vient de lever le voile sur sa nouvelle 10+, une voiture électrique taillée pour la performance. Version sportive de la Z10 restylée, elle affiche une puissance particulièrement élevée.

item.title: Microsoft exec promises big changes for Windows 11 will come 'this month and every month this year' — but don't get too excited
item.description: Microsoft seems to be serious about fixing Windows 11 quickly, as exec promises big changes will come 'this month and every month this year'.

item.title: Voitures électriques : Xpeng rejoint BYD et Leapmotor dans le club très fermé des constructeurs chinois rentables
item.description: Ça y est, après plus de dix ans d’existence, Xpeng rejoint le club très fermé des constructeurs automobiles chinois rentables. La firme dédiée aux voitures électriques vient d’enregistrer des bénéfices pour la première fois.

item.title: Here Are Five Great Deals to Not Miss Dreame’s Spring Sale, Vacuums and Hair Dryers Included
item.description: Four elite-level cleaning machines and one amazing hair dryer are the stars of this weeklong sale event, with hundreds of dollars in price cuts.

item.title: Apple Plans pourrait diffuser de la publicité dès l’été 2026
item.description: Apple continue d’étendre sa stratégie publicitaire. Après l’App Store, c’est Apple Plans qui pourrait ajouter de la publicité quitte à dégrader son expérience utilisateur.

item.title: Philips remplace Google TV par Titan OS sur tous ses téléviseurs 2026
item.description: Philips, via son partenaire TP Vision, a confirmé que l’ensemble de ses téléviseurs 2026 abandonnera Google TV au profit de Titan OS, y compris sur les séries OLED les plus haut de gamme. C’est la fin de plusieurs années de collaboration avec l’écosystème de Google.

item.title: « Le coût de nos véhicules est trop élevé » : le terrible aveu de Volkswagen pour sauver ses voitures électriques
item.description: Encore en pleine crise, Volkswagen veut désormais miser sur la Chine afin de réduire le coût de ses voitures électriques. Et ce alors que ses ventes sur ce territoire ont drastiquement chuté au fil des mois.

item.title: Microsoft a-t-il bloqué l’astuce pour accélérer les SSD sur Windows 11 ? Pas tout à fait…
item.description: Microsoft a désormais rendu impossible l'activation du nouveau pilote natif caché pour votre SSD. Mais il reste une manipulation possible si vous souhaitez prendre le risque.

item.title: L’arme des hackers contre la double authentification est déjà de retour
item.description: Les autorités pensaient avoir mis un terme aux activités de l’une des plateformes de phishing les plus dangereuses du moment, Tycoon2FA. En quelques jours, la plateforme, spécialisée dans le contournement de la double authentification, est revenue sur le devant de la scène… et elle tourne à plein régime.

item.title: Best Premium Soundbars: Dolby Atmos, Hi-Res Audio, and More
item.description: Soundbars just keep getting better, but some rise above the rest. These are the best high-end soundbars and wireless A/V speakers I've tried.

item.title: Chris Hayes Has Some Advice for Keeping Up With the News
item.description: The host of MS Now’s All In, knows how hard it is to stay current. But he also knows where you should focus your attention—and it starts with a sober view of AI.

item.title: Marvel's best bromance will return in Wonder Man season 2 — and the highly-rated Disney+ show's next entry needs to prove if a big MCU fan theory is right or not
item.description: Wonder Man is only the third live-action Marvel show to be renewed for a second season.

item.title: En pleine pénurie, il est possible de trouver 32 Go de RAM pour presque 30 % de moins
item.description: PC Componentes casse le prix de ces deux barrettes de RAM DDR5 Corsair Vengeance avec une remise de 28 %, soit une réduction de 130 euros sur leur prix d'origine.

item.title: Le paradoxe Tesla : pourquoi Elon Musk mise 3 milliards sur la Chine pour son solaire « Made in USA »
item.description: Le géant américain Tesla s’oriente vers la Chine pour se procurer des équipements destinés à la production de panneaux solaires « made in USA ». Avec l’intention d’étendre largement sa capacité, l’entreprise prévoit d’investir plusieurs milliards de dollars dans du matériel chinois.

item.title: Les Skoda Elroq et Enyaq reçoivent une nouvelle batterie et de nombreuses améliorations pour rester dans le coup face au Tesla Model Y
item.description: Les Skoda Elroq et Enyaq s'offrent une mise à jour de mi-carrière. Sans révolution visuelle, les évolutions portent surtout sur la batterie, le confort à bord et les aides à la conduite. De quoi rester dans la course sur un marché qui ne ralentit pas, surtout pour l'Elroq qui caracole en tête des ventes de voitures électriques en Europe depuis le début de l'année. ...

item.title: 'Very significant privacy concerns': UK hands over financial regulation data to Palantir to help tackle financial crime
item.description: The FCA is giving Palantir a three-month trial to see if it's the right partner to work on processing the body's huge amounts of data.

item.title: Amazons Big Spring Sale is back: The best deals already live on Apple, robot vacuums, headphones, and more
item.description: Amazon's Big Spring Sale is back from March 25 to 31. Shop the best early deals on Apple, headphones, TVs, and more.

item.title: Intel (enfin) sur les pas d’AMD ? Le socket LGA-1954 pourrait devenir multi-générations
item.description: Dans un entretien accordé à la presse spécialisée, Robert Hallock, General Manager d'Intel, a laissé entendre que la firme pourrait enfin arrêter de changer de socket à chaque génération de processeur de bureau ou presque. Une déclaration bien trop timide à ce stade, mais qui va indiscutablement dans le bon sens.

item.title: From hype to value: The AI trends set to shape 2026
item.description: 2026 will see AI move from pilots to accountable production at scale, delivering real value.

item.title: « Moins chère qu’un vélo » : comment la Leapmotor T03 a braqué le marché européen grâce à Stellantis et l’Italie
item.description: La Leapmotor T03 vendue par Stellantis en Europe a connu une belle envolée au mois de février en Europe. Et cela notamment grâce à une forte baisse de son prix en Italie. Là-bas, la voiture électrique chinoise serait même moins chère qu’un vélo haut de gamme.

item.title: Apple WWDC 2026 is official, with iOS 27 and AI advancements on the menu
item.description: Apple has officially announced the dates for its Worldwide Developers Conference, which kicks off on June 8.

item.title: Realme Buds Air8 : la réduction de bruit à bas coût
item.description: Les realme Buds Air8 viennent d’être officialisés. Au menu, une grosse réduction de bruit et une partie audio soignée pour un prix doux.

item.title: Figure AI Founder and iPhone Air Designer Team Up on AI Mystery Product
item.description: A new company called Hark is building an ambitious-sounding AI lab, with a physical product of some sort in the works.

item.title: Your Body Is Betraying Your Right to Privacy
item.description: Attachment to smart devices and biometric surveillance leaves Americans more vulnerable to police searches than ever. Left unchecked it will only get worse.

item.title: ‘Get Down! Get Down! They’re Gonna See Us!’: Six Months of Hiding From ICE
item.description: A family in Chicago has been terrified to leave their apartment. Agents could be anywhere.

item.title: Ultrahuman ramps up U.S. push with Ring Pro as Oura tightens its grip
item.description: Ultrahuman pushes back into the U.S. with Ring Pro, as Oura strengthens its lead in a market driving 60% of global demand.

item.title: From the Backlog
item.description: All TechRadar stories tagged From the Backlog

item.title: The best deals this week, according to Mashables team of shopping experts
item.description: The best deals this week. Mashable's shoping team finds deals on AirPods, Pokémon cards, robot vacuums, and so much more.

item.title: Build apps faster with Microsoft Visual Studio Professional 2026 for under $50
item.description: Get Microsoft Visual Studio Professional 2026 for $42.49 with code MARCH15, helping you building apps across platforms with AI-assisted coding tools.
